text.title: Weak-Model Preselection Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the weak-model-preselection methodology end-to-end.

step.name: audit-noise-ratio
step.action: Measure average input token count + estimated relevant-content ratio (manual or heuristic).
step.decision-gate: If noise ratio < 70%, skip — cascade overhead not justified.

step.name: pick-cascade-variant
step.action: Filter (boolean keep), Extract (paths), Classify (category), Rank (top-K). Each maps to a variant.

step.name: label-and-eval
step.input: 100+ labeled examples (ground-truth keep_flag / paths / category / top-K)
step.action: Run weak model; compute recall + precision. Tune threshold / few-shot until recall ≥ 0.95.
step.decision-gate: If recall plateaus < 0.95 after 3 tuning rounds, switch to a less aggressive variant (rank instead of filter).

step.name: wire-escape-hatch
step.action: Add a confidence-threshold check on weak-model output. If below threshold OR output empty, route to strong model with the full original input.

step.name: measure-savings-and-deploy
step.input: Production traffic mirror or staging eval set
step.action: Run cascade vs single-pass strong-model baseline. Compute cost_savings_ratio. Deploy if ≥ 3x.
step.output: Cost-savings report; cascade live behind feature flag
step.decision-gate: If ratio < 3x, document the negative result and revert.
